data.narrative:
Castelion is a Series B defense and security company founded by Bryon Hargis, Sean Pitt, and Andrew Kreitz, currently valued at $1.0B+ on $100M raised to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms operating in the same sector and stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier from the last post-money valuation of $1.0B, yielding $1.9B. Peer-median benchmarking across 7 same-sector, same-stage comparables—including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, and Harmattan AI—establishes a median valuation of $1.2B, with a peer range of $300M to $1.5B. The peer sample provides empirical grounding in observable market data from recent funding events.

The two methods converge on a triangulated valuation range of $1.1B (low) to $2.2B (high), with a median estimate of $1.6B. This range reflects the step-up methodology's higher implied value against the more conservative peer-median approach. Key uncertainties include the specific competitive positioning relative to higher-valued peers like Vannevar Labs ($1.5B) and whether Castelion's capital efficiency and progress metrics justify placement toward the median or upper quartile of the peer set. Additional revenue and growth trajectory data would refine this estimate.
